title: Remove the Tip or A-Axis Cable
summary: Safely remove the existing tip or A-axis cable from the tipper by locking out the equipment, gaining access to the cable path, disconnecting the four M8 connectors, opening the cable carrier, documenting and cutting cable ties, unplugging the cable from the A-axis PCA, and preserving cable markings for replacement.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the documented maintenance task is to remove the existing Tip or A-Axis cable so it can be replaced on the tipper assembly.
not_for: Situations where the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure is unavailable or cannot be completed
Tasks requiring installation of the replacement cable; this runbook covers removal only

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, remove covers and access panels as needed, disconnect connectors, document cable routing and tie placement, remove the cable from the carrier, and preserve markings for replacement.

safety_notes: This procedure is not support-safe for general operator use.
LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before beginning removal.
Preserve routing and tie-position evidence by marking the cable and taking a picture before removal.
access_or_tools_needed: 3/8-in. wrench or socket if side panel removal is necessary
Camera or phone to take a picture of cable tie placement

system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure

visual_references.description: Tip Cable figure associated with the removal procedure.
Tip Cable Routing figure for identifying cable path and routing context.
Page 166 maintenance diagram associated with M8 connector disconnection, grommet pass-through, cable carrier opening, tie locations, and A-axis PCA disconnection.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe maintenance state before cable removal.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before performing any removal work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in the required lockout/tagout state for maintenance.
steps.healthy_condition: Lockout/tagout has been completed per the referenced procedure and maintenance access can proceed safely.
steps.failure_condition: The lockout/tagout procedure is unavailable, cannot be completed, or the tipper cannot be placed in a safe maintenance state.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced lockout/tagout procedure is not available
Lockout/tagout cannot be completed

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
The removal procedure instructs the technician to LOTO the tipper.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor and cable removal area when the side panel blocks access.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The side panel is removed when needed and the motor access area is exposed.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is removed without damage and the required access area is visible.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel or fasteners cannot be removed as described, or access to the motor area is still blocked.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Side panel access context for the Tip Cable removal procedure.
Cable routing context near the motor access area after side panel removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed as described
Access to the motor or cable path cannot be obtained

source_evidence.text: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping screws securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
The removal procedure instructs the technician to remove nine self-tapping screws from the side panel if needed to access the motor.
The surrounding removal steps instruct the technician to remove the station side panel to access the motor.

steps.title: Remove the valve cover
steps.purpose: Open access to the cable and connector area behind the valve cover.
steps.instruction: Remove the valve cover by removing the four M4x50 socket-head screws. Verify the correct cover and fasteners before removal.
steps.expected_result: The valve cover is removed and the covered cable area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct valve cover and four M4x50 socket-head screws are identified and removed cleanly.
steps.failure_condition: The correct cover or fasteners cannot be identified, or the cover cannot be removed as described.

actions.label: Remove valve cover screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the four M4x50 socket-head screws securing the valve cover.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Valve cover area associated with the Tip Cable removal procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct cover and fasteners cannot be identified
The valve cover cannot be removed as described

source_evidence.text: Remove the valve cover by removing the four M4x50 socket-head screws.
The removal procedure instructs the technician to remove the valve cover using four M4x50 socket-head screws.

steps.title: Disconnect the four M8 connectors
steps.purpose: Free the cable from its external connector terminations before pulling it through the grommet and carrier.
steps.expected_result: All four M8 connectors are disconnected from the cable assembly.
steps.healthy_condition: All four M8 connectors are fully disconnected and available to be fed through the grommet.
steps.failure_condition: One or more M8 connectors cannot be accessed or disconnected as described.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The four M8 connectors referenced in the removal procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any M8 connector cannot be accessed
Any M8 connector cannot be disconnected as described

source_evidence.text: Disconnect all four M8 connectors.
Nearby procedure text references disconnecting all four M8 connectors.

steps.title: Feed the connectors through the grommet
steps.purpose: Remove the cable from the grommet opening without damaging the connector ends.
steps.instruction: Pull the jacketed part of the cable out of the grommet, then feed the M8 connectors through the grommet one at a time.
steps.expected_result: The cable jacket and all four M8 connectors are passed through the grommet opening.
steps.healthy_condition: The jacketed cable is free of the grommet and the connectors pass through one at a time without obstruction.
steps.failure_condition: The cable cannot be pulled from the grommet, or the connectors cannot be fed through as described.

actions.label: Pull cable jacket from grommet
actions.exact_action: Pull the jacketed part of the cable out of the grommet.

actions.label: Feed connectors through grommet
actions.exact_action: Feed the M8 connectors through the grommet one at a time.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The grommet where the jacketed cable passes through and the connector pass-through path.

stop_or_escalate_if: The cable cannot be removed from the grommet
The connectors cannot be fed through the grommet one at a time

source_evidence.text: Pull the jacketed part of the cable out of the grommet, then feed the M8 connectors through the grommet one at a time.
Nearby procedure text references feeding the connectors through the grommet one at a time.

steps.title: Open the cable carrier cross bars
steps.purpose: Expose the cable inside the carrier so it can be marked and removed.
steps.instruction: Open all cross bars on the outside of the cable carrier by snapping them open.
steps.expected_result: The cable carrier cross bars are open and the cable is accessible along the carrier path.
steps.healthy_condition: All required cross bars are open and the cable can be seen and reached inside the carrier.
steps.failure_condition: The cross bars cannot be opened or the cable remains inaccessible.

actions.label: Open cable carrier cross bars
actions.exact_action: Open all cross bars on the outside of the cable carrier by snapping them open.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cable carrier cross bars that snap open on the outside of the carrier.

stop_or_escalate_if: The cable carrier cross bars cannot be opened
The cable remains inaccessible after opening the carrier

source_evidence.text: Open all cross bars on the outside of the cable carrier by snapping them open.
Nearby procedure text references opening the snap-open cross bars on the cable carrier.

steps.title: Mark the cable at both carrier entry points
steps.purpose: Preserve routing position information for installation of the replacement cable.
steps.instruction: Use tape or a marker to mark the cable on both ends where the cable enters the cable carrier.
steps.expected_result: The cable is clearly marked at both ends where it enters the cable carrier.
steps.healthy_condition: Both cable entry locations are visibly marked and can be transferred to the replacement cable later.
steps.failure_condition: The cable cannot be marked clearly or the entry locations cannot be identified.

actions.label: Mark cable entry points
actions.exact_action: Use tape or a marker to mark the cable on both ends where the cable enters the cable carrier.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cable routing path and carrier entry locations to preserve marking positions.
Cable carrier entry points where the cable should be marked on both ends.

stop_or_escalate_if: The cable entry points cannot be identified
Markings cannot be applied or preserved

source_evidence.text: Use tape or a marker to mark the cable on both ends where the cable enters the cable carrier.
Nearby procedure text references marking where the cable enters the carrier on both ends.

steps.title: Document and cut the cable ties
steps.purpose: Preserve tie placement information and free the cable from the carrier and carriage tie points.
steps.instruction: Note how the cable ties are situated at both ends of the carrier, take a picture, and cut the cable ties holding the cable to the carrier at both ends and at the tie point on the A-axis carriage.
steps.expected_result: Tie placement is documented and the cable is released from the documented tie points.
steps.healthy_condition: A clear record of tie placement exists and the cable is free at both carrier ends and the A-axis carriage tie point.
steps.failure_condition: Tie placement cannot be documented, or one or more cable ties cannot be cut or removed as described.

actions.label: Inspect tie placement
actions.exact_action: Note how the cable ties are situated at both ends of the carrier.

actions.label: Photograph tie placement
actions.exact_action: Take a picture of the cable tie placement before removal.

actions.label: Cut carrier and carriage ties
actions.exact_action: Cut the cable ties holding the cable to the carrier at both ends and at the tie point on the A-axis carriage.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cable routing and likely tie placement context along the carrier.
Cable tie locations at both ends of the carrier and the tie point on the A-axis carriage.

stop_or_escalate_if: Tie placement cannot be documented before removal
Cable ties cannot be cut or accessed as described

source_evidence.text: Note how the cable ties are situated at both ends of the carrier, take a picture, and cut the cable ties holding the cable to the carrier at both ends and at the tie point on the A-axis carriage.
Nearby procedure text references documenting and cutting cable ties at both ends and at the A-axis carriage tie point.
Preserve routing and tie-position evidence by marking the cable and taking a picture before removal.

steps.title: Unplug the cable from the A-axis PCA and remove it
steps.purpose: Fully disconnect the cable from the A-axis PCA and remove it from the cable carrier.
steps.instruction: Unplug the cable from the A-axis PCA and remove the cable from the cable carrier. Cut one more cable tie near the PCA if present.
steps.expected_result: The cable is unplugged from the A-axis PCA and fully removed from the cable carrier and tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The cable is completely free from the A-axis PCA, carrier, and any remaining tie point near the PCA.
steps.failure_condition: The A-axis PCA connection cannot be unplugged, the cable cannot be removed from the carrier, or a remaining tie near the PCA cannot be cleared.

actions.label: Unplug from A-axis PCA
actions.exact_action: Unplug the cable from the A-axis PCA.

actions.label: Remove cable from carrier
actions.exact_action: Remove the cable from the cable carrier.

actions.label: Cut remaining tie near PCA if present
actions.exact_action: Cut one more cable tie near the PCA if present.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Cable connection at the A-axis PCA and nearby tie location.

stop_or_escalate_if: The cable cannot be unplugged from the A-axis PCA
The cable cannot be removed from the cable carrier
A tie near the PCA prevents removal and cannot be cleared

source_evidence.text: Unplug the cable from the A-axis PCA and remove the cable from the cable carrier. Cut one more cable tie near the PCA if present.
Nearby procedure text references unplugging the cable from the A-axis PCA.

steps.title: Transfer the cable markings to the new cable
steps.purpose: Preserve routing reference information for installation of the replacement cable.
steps.instruction: Transfer the cable markings to the new cable.
steps.expected_result: The replacement cable carries the same routing reference markings as the removed cable.
steps.healthy_condition: The new cable is marked to match the removed cable's carrier entry references.
steps.failure_condition: The original markings are missing, unclear, or cannot be transferred to the new cable.

actions.label: Transfer markings
actions.exact_action: Transfer the cable markings to the new cable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Routing context that the transferred markings are intended to preserve.

stop_or_escalate_if: The original cable markings are not available or are unclear
The replacement cable cannot be marked to preserve routing reference

source_evidence.text: Transfer the cable markings to the new cable.
Step numbering in source says transfer markings from Step 5, but the marking action appears in source Step 7; preserved intent without correcting the manual.

success_criteria: The existing tip or A-axis cable is fully removed from the tipper.
The cable is disconnected from all four M8 connectors and the A-axis PCA.
The cable has been removed from the grommet and cable carrier.
Routing and marking information has been preserved for installation of the replacement cable.
healthy_conditions: Tipper is successfully locked out/tagged out before removal begins.
Required access covers and panels can be removed as described.
All four M8 connectors disconnect cleanly.
Cable carrier cross bars open and the cable can be accessed.
Cable tie placement is documented before ties are cut.
The cable is fully removed and markings are transferred to the new cable.
failure_conditions: The referenced lockout/tagout procedure is unavailable or cannot be completed.
The side panel, valve cover, connectors, cable carrier, or A-axis PCA connection cannot be accessed or removed as described.
Cable routing or tie-position evidence is not preserved before removal.
The cable cannot be fully removed from the tipper.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the referenced lockout/tagout procedure is not available or cannot be completed.
Escalate if the side panel, valve cover, connectors, cable carrier, or A-axis PCA connection cannot be accessed or removed as described.
Stop and preserve evidence if cable routing or tie placement cannot be documented before removal.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall Tip Cable figure associated with this removal procedure.
Cable routing reference for preserving path and marking positions.
M8 connectors, grommet pass-through, cable carrier, tie points, and A-axis PCA disconnection area.
